---
title: "1.4 Assignment and Input"
subtitle: "Part 1: Assignment Statements and Tracing"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Assignment Statements

### Initialize or change a variable

<span class="term">Assignment statements</span> (赋值语句) initialize or change the value stored in a variable using the assignment operator `=`.

An assignment statement always has a single variable on the left hand side.

The value of the <span class="term">expression</span> on the right of the `=` sign is assigned to and stored in the variable on the left side.

## Assignment Statement

### `variable = expression;`

::: {.image-fit}
![Assignment Statement: variable equals expression](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-4-assignment-and-input/assignment.png){fig-align="center" width="40%"}
:::

Instead of saying equals for the `=` in an assignment statement, say <span class="mark">gets</span> or <span class="mark">is assigned</span> to remember that the variable gets the value on the right.

## Assignment Example

### Copy of another variable value

We can set one variable's value to a <span class="mark">copy</span> of the value of another variable like `y = x;`.

This will not change the value of the variable that you are copying from.

External visualizer/video links are not required in this static deck; the same trace is preserved below.

## Trace Task

### `codelens:: asgn_viz1`

Step through the following code to see how the values of the variables change.

## Code Window

### `codelens:: asgn_viz1`

```java
public class Test2
{
  public static void main(String[] args)
  {
    int x = 3;
    int y = 2;
    System.out.println(x);
    System.out.println(y);
    x = y;
    System.out.println(x);
    System.out.println(y);
    y = 5;
    System.out.println(x);
    System.out.println(y);
  }
}
```

## Trace Focus

### `codelens:: asgn_viz1`

Trace these moments in order:

::: {.tight-list}
- after `int x = 3;`
- after `int y = 2;`
- after `x = y;`
- after `y = 5;`
:::

After `x = y;`, changing `y` later does not change `x`.

## Check Your Understanding

### `mchoice:: qasgn1`

What are the values of `x`, `y`, and `z` after the following code executes?

```java
int x = 0;
int y = 1;
int z = 2;
x = y;
y = y * 2;
z = 3;
```

## Answer Reasoning

### `mchoice:: qasgn1`

::: {.tight-list}
- A. `x = 0, y = 1, z = 2`
- B. `x = 1, y = 2, z = 3`
- C. `x = 2, y = 2, z = 3`
- D. `x = 0, y = 0, z = 3`
:::

Correct answer: <span class="mark">B. `x = 1, y = 2, z = 3`</span>

`x` changes to `y`'s initial value, `y`'s value is doubled, and `z` is set to `3`.

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: swapParsons`

Textbook prompt: The following has the correct code to "swap" the values in `x` and `y`, but the code is mixed up and contains one extra block which is not needed in a correct solution.

Drag the needed blocks into the correct order.

## Mixed Blocks

### `parsonsprob:: swapParsons`

```java
int x = 3;
int y = 5;
int temp = 0;
temp = x;
x = y;
y = temp;
```

Extra block:

```java
y = x;
```

## Correct Order

### `parsonsprob:: swapParsons`

```java
int x = 3;
int y = 5;
int temp = 0;
temp = x;
x = y;
y = temp;
```

The extra variable `temp` is needed because assignment overwrites the old value.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- define assignment as storing the right-side expression value in the left-side variable
- read `=` as "gets" or "is assigned" in assignment statements
- trace copied values without treating `=` as mathematical equality
- explain why changing `y` after `x = y;` does not change `x`
- use a temporary variable to swap two values safely
:::

## End

### Continue to Part 2

[Next: Data types, null, increment, and input →](1-4-part-2-types-null-increment-and-input.html)
